<!-- Code generated by gen-cdp-tools; DO NOT EDIT. -->
# Code-generated CDP tools

Source: [`cdp/manifest.yaml`](../cdp/manifest.yaml) + vendored protocol JSON in [`cdp/protocol/`](../cdp/protocol/).
Run `go generate ./...` to refresh after edits to either.

Conventions:
- Every tool name has the shape `cdp.<lowerDomain>.<lowerMethod>`.
- Target-scoped tools accept optional `targetId` / `urlPattern` selectors.
- Tools marked **dangerous** refuse unless the dispatcher was started with `--allow-dangerous-cdp` (or `OAMCP_ALLOW_DANGEROUS_CDP=1`).
- Tools marked **binary** accept an optional `outputPath`; setting it redirects the base64 payload to disk and returns `{path,sizeBytes,mimeType}`.

## Accessibility — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.accessibility.getAXNodeAndAncestors` Fetches a node and all ancestors up to and including the root. Requires `enable()` to have been called previously.
- `cdp.accessibility.getChildAXNodes` Fetches a particular accessibility node by AXNodeId. Requires `enable()` to have been called previously.
- `cdp.accessibility.getFullAXTree` Fetches the entire accessibility tree for the root Document
- `cdp.accessibility.getPartialAXTree` Fetches the accessibility node and partial accessibility tree for this DOM node, if it exists.
- `cdp.accessibility.getRootAXNode` Fetches the root node. Requires `enable()` to have been called previously.
- `cdp.accessibility.queryAXTree` Query a DOM node's accessibility subtree for accessible name and role. This command computes the name and role for all nodes in the subtree, including those that are ignored for accessibility, and returns those that match the specified name and role. If no DOM node is specified, or the DOM node does not exist, the command returns an error. If neither `accessibleName` or `role` is specified, it returns all the accessibility nodes in the subtree.

## Animation — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.animation.getCurrentTime` Returns the current time of the an animation.
- `cdp.animation.getPlaybackRate` Gets the playback rate of the document timeline.
- `cdp.animation.releaseAnimations` Releases a set of animations to no longer be manipulated.
- `cdp.animation.resolveAnimation` Gets the remote object of the Animation.
- `cdp.animation.seekAnimations` Seek a set of animations to a particular time within each animation.
- `cdp.animation.setPaused` Sets the paused state of a set of animations.
- `cdp.animation.setPlaybackRate` Sets the playback rate of the document timeline.
- `cdp.animation.setTiming` Sets the timing of an animation node.

## BackgroundService — scope: browser

- `cdp.backgroundService.clearEvents` Clears all stored data for the service.
- `cdp.backgroundService.setRecording` Set the recording state for the service.
- `cdp.backgroundService.startObserving` Enables event updates for the service.
- `cdp.backgroundService.stopObserving` Disables event updates for the service.

## Browser — scope: browser

- `cdp.browser.cancelDownload` Cancel a download if in progress
- `cdp.browser.close` — **dangerous** Close browser gracefully.
- `cdp.browser.crash` — **dangerous** Crashes browser on the main thread.
- `cdp.browser.crashGpuProcess` — **dangerous** Crashes GPU process.
- `cdp.browser.executeBrowserCommand` — **dangerous** Invoke custom browser commands used by telemetry.
- `cdp.browser.getBrowserCommandLine` Returns the command line switches for the browser process if, and only if --enable-automation is on the commandline.
- `cdp.browser.getVersion` Returns version information.
- `cdp.browser.getWindowBounds` Get position and size of the browser window.
- `cdp.browser.getWindowForTarget` Get the browser window that contains the devtools target.
- `cdp.browser.grantPermissions` Grant specific permissions to the given origin and reject all others. Deprecated. Use setPermission instead.
- `cdp.browser.resetPermissions` Reset all permission management for all origins.
- `cdp.browser.setContentsSize` Set size of the browser contents resizing browser window as necessary.
- `cdp.browser.setDockTile` Set dock tile details, platform-specific.
- `cdp.browser.setDownloadBehavior` Set the behavior when downloading a file.
- `cdp.browser.setPermission` Set permission settings for given embedding and embedded origins.
- `cdp.browser.setWindowBounds` Set position and/or size of the browser window.

## CSS — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.cSS.addRule` Inserts a new rule with the given `ruleText` in a stylesheet with given `styleSheetId`, at the position specified by `location`.
- `cdp.cSS.collectClassNames` Returns all class names from specified stylesheet.
- `cdp.cSS.createStyleSheet` Creates a new special "via-inspector" stylesheet in the frame with given `frameId`.
- `cdp.cSS.forcePseudoState` Ensures that the given node will have specified pseudo-classes whenever its style is computed by the browser.
- `cdp.cSS.forceStartingStyle` Ensures that the given node is in its starting-style state.
- `cdp.cSS.getAnimatedStylesForNode` Returns the styles coming from animations & transitions including the animation & transition styles coming from inheritance chain.
- `cdp.cSS.getBackgroundColors`
- `cdp.cSS.getComputedStyleForNode` Returns the computed style for a DOM node identified by `nodeId`.
- `cdp.cSS.getEnvironmentVariables` Returns the values of the default UA-defined environment variables used in env()
- `cdp.cSS.getInlineStylesForNode` Returns the styles defined inline (explicitly in the "style" attribute and implicitly, using DOM attributes) for a DOM node identified by `nodeId`.
- `cdp.cSS.getLayersForNode` Returns all layers parsed by the rendering engine for the tree scope of a node. Given a DOM element identified by nodeId, getLayersForNode returns the root layer for the nearest ancestor document or shadow root. The layer root contains the full layer tree for the tree scope and their ordering.
- `cdp.cSS.getLocationForSelector` Given a CSS selector text and a style sheet ID, getLocationForSelector returns an array of locations of the CSS selector in the style sheet.
- `cdp.cSS.getLonghandProperties`
- `cdp.cSS.getMatchedStylesForNode` Returns requested styles for a DOM node identified by `nodeId`.
- `cdp.cSS.getMediaQueries` Returns all media queries parsed by the rendering engine.
- `cdp.cSS.getPlatformFontsForNode` Requests information about platform fonts which we used to render child TextNodes in the given node.
- `cdp.cSS.getStyleSheetText` Returns the current textual content for a stylesheet.
- `cdp.cSS.resolveValues` Resolve the specified values in the context of the provided element. For example, a value of '1em' is evaluated according to the computed 'font-size' of the element and a value 'calc(1px + 2px)' will be resolved to '3px'. If the `propertyName` was specified the `values` are resolved as if they were property's declaration. If a value cannot be parsed according to the provided property syntax, the value is parsed using combined syntax as if null `propertyName` was provided. If the value cannot be resolved even then, return the provided value without any changes. Note: this function currently does not resolve CSS random() function, it returns unmodified random() function parts.`
- `cdp.cSS.setContainerQueryText` Modifies the expression of a container query.
- `cdp.cSS.setEffectivePropertyValueForNode` Find a rule with the given active property for the given node and set the new value for this property
- `cdp.cSS.setKeyframeKey` Modifies the keyframe rule key text.
- `cdp.cSS.setLocalFontsEnabled` Enables/disables rendering of local CSS fonts (enabled by default).
- `cdp.cSS.setMediaText` Modifies the rule selector.
- `cdp.cSS.setNavigationText` Modifies the expression of a navigation at-rule.
- `cdp.cSS.setPropertyRulePropertyName` Modifies the property rule property name.
- `cdp.cSS.setRuleSelector` Modifies the rule selector.
- `cdp.cSS.setScopeText` Modifies the expression of a scope at-rule.
- `cdp.cSS.setStyleSheetText` Sets the new stylesheet text.
- `cdp.cSS.setStyleTexts` Applies specified style edits one after another in the given order.
- `cdp.cSS.setSupportsText` Modifies the expression of a supports at-rule.
- `cdp.cSS.startRuleUsageTracking` Enables the selector recording.
- `cdp.cSS.stopRuleUsageTracking` Stop tracking rule usage and return the list of rules that were used since last call to `takeCoverageDelta` (or since start of coverage instrumentation).
- `cdp.cSS.takeComputedStyleUpdates` Polls the next batch of computed style updates.
- `cdp.cSS.takeCoverageDelta` Obtain list of rules that became used since last call to this method (or since start of coverage instrumentation).
- `cdp.cSS.trackComputedStyleUpdates` Starts tracking the given computed styles for updates. The specified array of properties replaces the one previously specified. Pass empty array to disable tracking. Use takeComputedStyleUpdates to retrieve the list of nodes that had properties modified. The changes to computed style properties are only tracked for nodes pushed to the front-end by the DOM agent. If no changes to the tracked properties occur after the node has been pushed to the front-end, no updates will be issued for the node.
- `cdp.cSS.trackComputedStyleUpdatesForNode` Starts tracking the given node for the computed style updates and whenever the computed style is updated for node, it queues a `computedStyleUpdated` event with throttling. There can only be 1 node tracked for computed style updates so passing a new node id removes tracking from the previous node. Pass `undefined` to disable tracking.

## CacheStorage — scope: browser

- `cdp.cacheStorage.deleteCache` Deletes a cache.
- `cdp.cacheStorage.deleteEntry` Deletes a cache entry.
- `cdp.cacheStorage.requestCacheNames` Requests cache names.
- `cdp.cacheStorage.requestCachedResponse` Fetches cache entry.
- `cdp.cacheStorage.requestEntries` Requests data from cache.

## DOM — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.dOM.collectClassNamesFromSubtree` Collects class names for the node with given id and all of it's child nodes.
- `cdp.dOM.copyTo` Creates a deep copy of the specified node and places it into the target container before the given anchor.
- `cdp.dOM.describeNode` Describes node given its id, does not require domain to be enabled. Does not start tracking any objects, can be used for automation.
- `cdp.dOM.discardSearchResults` Discards search results from the session with the given id. `getSearchResults` should no longer be called for that search.
- `cdp.dOM.focus` Focuses the given element.
- `cdp.dOM.forceShowPopover` When enabling, this API force-opens the popover identified by nodeId and keeps it open until disabled.
- `cdp.dOM.getAnchorElement` Returns the target anchor element of the given anchor query according to https://www.w3.org/TR/css-anchor-position-1/#target.
- `cdp.dOM.getAttributes` Returns attributes for the specified node.
- `cdp.dOM.getBoxModel` Returns boxes for the given node.
- `cdp.dOM.getContainerForNode` Returns the query container of the given node based on container query conditions: containerName, physical and logical axes, and whether it queries scroll-state or anchored elements. If no axes are provided and queriesScrollState is false, the style container is returned, which is the direct parent or the closest element with a matching container-name.
- `cdp.dOM.getContentQuads` Returns quads that describe node position on the page. This method might return multiple quads for inline nodes.
- `cdp.dOM.getDetachedDomNodes` Returns list of detached nodes
- `cdp.dOM.getDocument` Returns the root DOM node (and optionally the subtree) to the caller. Implicitly enables the DOM domain events for the current target.
- `cdp.dOM.getElementByRelation` Returns the NodeId of the matched element according to certain relations.
- `cdp.dOM.getFileInfo` Returns file information for the given File wrapper.
- `cdp.dOM.getFlattenedDocument` Returns the root DOM node (and optionally the subtree) to the caller. Deprecated, as it is not designed to work well with the rest of the DOM agent. Use DOMSnapshot.captureSnapshot instead.
- `cdp.dOM.getFrameOwner` Returns iframe node that owns iframe with the given domain.
- `cdp.dOM.getNodeForLocation` Returns node id at given location. Depending on whether DOM domain is enabled, nodeId is either returned or not.
- `cdp.dOM.getNodeStackTraces` Gets stack traces associated with a Node. As of now, only provides stack trace for Node creation.
- `cdp.dOM.getNodesForSubtreeByStyle` Finds nodes with a given computed style in a subtree.
- `cdp.dOM.getOuterHTML` Returns node's HTML markup.
- `cdp.dOM.getQueryingDescendantsForContainer` Returns the descendants of a container query container that have container queries against this container.
- `cdp.dOM.getRelayoutBoundary` Returns the id of the nearest ancestor that is a relayout boundary.
- `cdp.dOM.getSearchResults` Returns search results from given `fromIndex` to given `toIndex` from the search with the given identifier.
- `cdp.dOM.getTopLayerElements` Returns NodeIds of current top layer elements. Top layer is rendered closest to the user within a viewport, therefore its elements always appear on top of all other content.
- `cdp.dOM.hideHighlight` Hides any highlight.
- `cdp.dOM.highlightNode` Highlights DOM node.
- `cdp.dOM.highlightRect` Highlights given rectangle.
- `cdp.dOM.markUndoableState` Marks last undoable state.
- `cdp.dOM.moveTo` Moves node into the new container, places it before the given anchor.
- `cdp.dOM.performSearch` Searches for a given string in the DOM tree. Use `getSearchResults` to access search results or `cancelSearch` to end this search session.
- `cdp.dOM.pushNodeByPathToFrontend` Requests that the node is sent to the caller given its path. // FIXME, use XPath
- `cdp.dOM.pushNodesByBackendIdsToFrontend` Requests that a batch of nodes is sent to the caller given their backend node ids.
- `cdp.dOM.querySelector` Executes `querySelector` on a given node.
- `cdp.dOM.querySelectorAll` Executes `querySelectorAll` on a given node.
- `cdp.dOM.redo` Re-does the last undone action.
- `cdp.dOM.removeAttribute` Removes attribute with given name from an element with given id.
- `cdp.dOM.removeNode` Removes node with given id.
- `cdp.dOM.requestChildNodes` Requests that children of the node with given id are returned to the caller in form of `setChildNodes` events where not only immediate children are retrieved, but all children down to the specified depth.
- `cdp.dOM.requestNode` Requests that the node is sent to the caller given the JavaScript node object reference. All nodes that form the path from the node to the root are also sent to the client as a series of `setChildNodes` notifications.
- `cdp.dOM.resolveNode` Resolves the JavaScript node object for a given NodeId or BackendNodeId.
- `cdp.dOM.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded` Scrolls the specified rect of the given node into view if not already visible. Note: exactly one between nodeId, backendNodeId and objectId should be passed to identify the node.
- `cdp.dOM.setAttributeValue` Sets attribute for an element with given id.
- `cdp.dOM.setAttributesAsText` Sets attributes on element with given id. This method is useful when user edits some existing attribute value and types in several attribute name/value pairs.
- `cdp.dOM.setFileInputFiles` Sets files for the given file input element.
- `cdp.dOM.setInspectedNode` Enables console to refer to the node with given id via $x (see Command Line API for more details $x functions).
- `cdp.dOM.setNodeName` Sets node name for a node with given id.
- `cdp.dOM.setNodeStackTracesEnabled` Sets if stack traces should be captured for Nodes. See `Node.getNodeStackTraces`. Default is disabled.
- `cdp.dOM.setNodeValue` Sets node value for a node with given id.
- `cdp.dOM.setOuterHTML` Sets node HTML markup, returns new node id.
- `cdp.dOM.undo` Undoes the last performed action.

## DOMDebugger — scope: target

- `cdp.dOMDebugger.getEventListeners` Returns event listeners of the given object.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.removeDOMBreakpoint` Removes DOM breakpoint that was set using `setDOMBreakpoint`.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.removeEventListenerBreakpoint` Removes breakpoint on particular DOM event.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.removeInstrumentationBreakpoint` Removes breakpoint on particular native event.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.removeXHRBreakpoint` Removes breakpoint from XMLHttpRequest.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.setBreakOnCSPViolation` Sets breakpoint on particular CSP violations.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.setDOMBreakpoint` Sets breakpoint on particular operation with DOM.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.setEventListenerBreakpoint` Sets breakpoint on particular DOM event.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.setInstrumentationBreakpoint` Sets breakpoint on particular native event.
- `cdp.dOMDebugger.setXHRBreakpoint` Sets breakpoint on XMLHttpRequest.

## Debugger — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.debugger.continueToLocation` Continues execution until specific location is reached.
- `cdp.debugger.disassembleWasmModule`
- `cdp.debugger.evaluateOnCallFrame` Evaluates expression on a given call frame.
- `cdp.debugger.getPossibleBreakpoints` Returns possible locations for breakpoint. scriptId in start and end range locations should be the same.
- `cdp.debugger.getScriptSource` Returns source for the script with given id.
- `cdp.debugger.getStackTrace` Returns stack trace with given `stackTraceId`.
- `cdp.debugger.getWasmBytecode` This command is deprecated. Use getScriptSource instead.
- `cdp.debugger.nextWasmDisassemblyChunk` Disassemble the next chunk of lines for the module corresponding to the stream. If disassembly is complete, this API will invalidate the streamId and return an empty chunk. Any subsequent calls for the now invalid stream will return errors.
- `cdp.debugger.pause` — **dangerous** Stops on the next JavaScript statement.
- `cdp.debugger.pauseOnAsyncCall`
- `cdp.debugger.removeBreakpoint` Removes JavaScript breakpoint.
- `cdp.debugger.restartFrame` Restarts particular call frame from the beginning. The old, deprecated behavior of `restartFrame` is to stay paused and allow further CDP commands after a restart was scheduled. This can cause problems with restarting, so we now continue execution immediatly after it has been scheduled until we reach the beginning of the restarted frame. To stay back-wards compatible, `restartFrame` now expects a `mode` parameter to be present. If the `mode` parameter is missing, `restartFrame` errors out. The various return values are deprecated and `callFrames` is always empty. Use the call frames from the `Debugger#paused` events instead, that fires once V8 pauses at the beginning of the restarted function.
- `cdp.debugger.resume` Resumes JavaScript execution.
- `cdp.debugger.searchInContent` Searches for given string in script content.
- `cdp.debugger.setAsyncCallStackDepth` Enables or disables async call stacks tracking.
- `cdp.debugger.setBlackboxExecutionContexts` Replace previous blackbox execution contexts with passed ones. Forces backend to skip stepping/pausing in scripts in these execution contexts. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful.
- `cdp.debugger.setBlackboxPatterns` Replace previous blackbox patterns with passed ones. Forces backend to skip stepping/pausing in scripts with url matching one of the patterns. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful.
- `cdp.debugger.setBlackboxedRanges` Makes backend skip steps in the script in blackboxed ranges. VM will try leave blacklisted scripts by performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful. Positions array contains positions where blackbox state is changed. First interval isn't blackboxed. Array should be sorted.
- `cdp.debugger.setBreakpoint` Sets JavaScript breakpoint at a given location.
- `cdp.debugger.setBreakpointByUrl` Sets JavaScript breakpoint at given location specified either by URL or URL regex. Once this command is issued, all existing parsed scripts will have breakpoints resolved and returned in `locations` property. Further matching script parsing will result in subsequent `breakpointResolved` events issued. This logical breakpoint will survive page reloads.
- `cdp.debugger.setBreakpointOnFunctionCall` Sets JavaScript breakpoint before each call to the given function. If another function was created from the same source as a given one, calling it will also trigger the breakpoint.
- `cdp.debugger.setBreakpointsActive` Activates / deactivates all breakpoints on the page.
- `cdp.debugger.setInstrumentationBreakpoint` Sets instrumentation breakpoint.
- `cdp.debugger.setPauseOnExceptions` Defines pause on exceptions state. Can be set to stop on all exceptions, uncaught exceptions, or caught exceptions, no exceptions. Initial pause on exceptions state is `none`.
- `cdp.debugger.setReturnValue` Changes return value in top frame. Available only at return break position.
- `cdp.debugger.setScriptSource` Edits JavaScript source live. In general, functions that are currently on the stack can not be edited with a single exception: If the edited function is the top-most stack frame and that is the only activation of that function on the stack. In this case the live edit will be successful and a `Debugger.restartFrame` for the top-most function is automatically triggered.
- `cdp.debugger.setSkipAllPauses` Makes page not interrupt on any pauses (breakpoint, exception, dom exception etc).
- `cdp.debugger.setVariableValue` Changes value of variable in a callframe. Object-based scopes are not supported and must be mutated manually.
- `cdp.debugger.stepInto` Steps into the function call.
- `cdp.debugger.stepOut` Steps out of the function call.
- `cdp.debugger.stepOver` Steps over the statement.

## Emulation — scope: target

- `cdp.emulation.addScreen` Add a new screen to the device. Only supported in headless mode.
- `cdp.emulation.canEmulate` Tells whether emulation is supported.
- `cdp.emulation.clearDeviceMetricsOverride` Clears the overridden device metrics.
- `cdp.emulation.clearDevicePostureOverride` Clears a device posture override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride() or setDevicePostureOverride() and starts using posture information from the platform again. Does nothing if no override is set.
- `cdp.emulation.clearDisplayFeaturesOverride` Clears the display features override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride() or setDisplayFeaturesOverride() and starts using display features from the platform again. Does nothing if no override is set.
- `cdp.emulation.clearGeolocationOverride` Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.
- `cdp.emulation.clearIdleOverride` Clears Idle state overrides.
- `cdp.emulation.getOverriddenSensorInformation`
- `cdp.emulation.getScreenInfos` Returns device's screen configuration. In headful mode, the physical screens configuration is returned, whereas in headless mode, a virtual headless screen configuration is provided instead.
- `cdp.emulation.removeScreen` Remove screen from the device. Only supported in headless mode.
- `cdp.emulation.resetPageScaleFactor` Requests that page scale factor is reset to initial values.
- `cdp.emulation.setAutoDarkModeOverride` Automatically render all web contents using a dark theme.
- `cdp.emulation.setAutomationOverride` Allows overriding the automation flag.
- `cdp.emulation.setCPUThrottlingRate` Enables CPU throttling to emulate slow CPUs.
- `cdp.emulation.setDataSaverOverride` Override the value of navigator.connection.saveData
- `cdp.emulation.setDefaultBackgroundColorOverride` Sets or clears an override of the default background color of the frame. This override is used if the content does not specify one.
- `cdp.emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride` Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height, window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and "device-width"/"device-height"-related CSS media query results).
- `cdp.emulation.setDevicePostureOverride` Start reporting the given posture value to the Device Posture API. This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().
- `cdp.emulation.setDisabledImageTypes`
- `cdp.emulation.setDisplayFeaturesOverride` Start using the given display features to pupulate the Viewport Segments API. This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().
- `cdp.emulation.setDocumentCookieDisabled`
- `cdp.emulation.setEmitTouchEventsForMouse`
- `cdp.emulation.setEmulatedMedia` Emulates the given media type or media feature for CSS media queries.
- `cdp.emulation.setEmulatedOSTextScale` Emulates the given OS text scale.
- `cdp.emulation.setEmulatedVisionDeficiency` Emulates the given vision deficiency.
- `cdp.emulation.setFocusEmulationEnabled` Enables or disables simulating a focused and active page.
- `cdp.emulation.setGeolocationOverride` Overrides the Geolocation Position or Error. Omitting latitude, longitude or accuracy emulates position unavailable.
- `cdp.emulation.setHardwareConcurrencyOverride`
- `cdp.emulation.setIdleOverride` Overrides the Idle state.
- `cdp.emulation.setLocaleOverride` Overrides default host system locale with the specified one.
- `cdp.emulation.setNavigatorOverrides` Overrides value returned by the javascript navigator object.
- `cdp.emulation.setPageScaleFactor` Sets a specified page scale factor.
- `cdp.emulation.setPressureDataOverride` Provides a given pressure data set that will be processed and eventually be delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.
- `cdp.emulation.setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled` Overrides a pressure source of a given type, as used by the Compute Pressure API, so that updates to PressureObserver.observe() are provided via setPressureStateOverride instead of being retrieved from platform-provided telemetry data.
- `cdp.emulation.setPressureStateOverride` TODO: OBSOLETE: To remove when setPressureDataOverride is merged. Provides a given pressure state that will be processed and eventually be delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.
- `cdp.emulation.setPrimaryScreen` Set primary screen. Only supported in headless mode. Note that this changes the coordinate system origin to the top-left of the new primary screen, updating the bounds and work areas of all existing screens accordingly.
- `cdp.emulation.setSafeAreaInsetsOverride` Overrides the values for env(safe-area-inset-*) and env(safe-area-max-inset-*). Unset values will cause the respective variables to be undefined, even if previously overridden.
- `cdp.emulation.setScriptExecutionDisabled` Switches script execution in the page.
- `cdp.emulation.setScrollbarsHidden`
- `cdp.emulation.setSensorOverrideEnabled` Overrides a platform sensor of a given type. If |enabled| is true, calls to Sensor.start() will use a virtual sensor as backend rather than fetching data from a real hardware sensor. Otherwise, existing virtual sensor-backend Sensor objects will fire an error event and new calls to Sensor.start() will attempt to use a real sensor instead.
- `cdp.emulation.setSensorOverrideReadings` Updates the sensor readings reported by a sensor type previously overridden by setSensorOverrideEnabled.
- `cdp.emulation.setSmallViewportHeightDifferenceOverride` Allows overriding the difference between the small and large viewport sizes, which determine the value of the `svh` and `lvh` unit, respectively. Only supported for top-level frames.
- `cdp.emulation.setTimezoneOverride` Overrides default host system timezone with the specified one.
- `cdp.emulation.setTouchEmulationEnabled` Enables touch on platforms which do not support them.
- `cdp.emulation.setUserAgentOverride` Allows overriding user agent with the given string. `userAgentMetadata` must be set for Client Hint headers to be sent.
- `cdp.emulation.setVirtualTimePolicy` Turns on virtual time for all frames (replacing real-time with a synthetic time source) and sets the current virtual time policy. Note this supersedes any previous time budget.
- `cdp.emulation.setVisibleSize` Resizes the frame/viewport of the page. Note that this does not affect the frame's container (e.g. browser window). Can be used to produce screenshots of the specified size. Not supported on Android.
- `cdp.emulation.updateScreen` Updates specified screen parameters. Only supported in headless mode.

## Fetch — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.fetch.continueRequest` Continues the request, optionally modifying some of its parameters.
- `cdp.fetch.continueResponse` Continues loading of the paused response, optionally modifying the response headers. If either responseCode or headers are modified, all of them must be present.
- `cdp.fetch.continueWithAuth` Continues a request supplying authChallengeResponse following authRequired event.
- `cdp.fetch.failRequest` Causes the request to fail with specified reason.
- `cdp.fetch.fulfillRequest` Provides response to the request.
- `cdp.fetch.getResponseBody` Causes the body of the response to be received from the server and returned as a single string. May only be issued for a request that is paused in the Response stage and is mutually exclusive with takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream. Calling other methods that affect the request or disabling fetch domain before body is received results in an undefined behavior. Note that the response body is not available for redirects. Requests paused in the _redirect received_ state may be differentiated by `responseCode` and presence of `location` response header, see comments to `requestPaused` for details.
- `cdp.fetch.takeResponseBodyAsStream` Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body. The request must be paused in the HeadersReceived stage. Note that after this command the request can't be continued as is -- client either needs to cancel it or to provide the response body. The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position is specified. This method is mutually exclusive with getResponseBody. Calling other methods that affect the request or disabling fetch domain before body is received results in an undefined behavior.

## Input — scope: target

- `cdp.input.cancelDragging` Cancels any active dragging in the page.
- `cdp.input.dispatchDragEvent` Dispatches a drag event into the page.
- `cdp.input.dispatchKeyEvent` Dispatches a key event to the page.
- `cdp.input.dispatchMouseEvent` Dispatches a mouse event to the page.
- `cdp.input.dispatchTouchEvent` Dispatches a touch event to the page.
- `cdp.input.emulateTouchFromMouseEvent` Emulates touch event from the mouse event parameters.
- `cdp.input.imeSetComposition` This method sets the current candidate text for IME. Use imeCommitComposition to commit the final text. Use imeSetComposition with empty string as text to cancel composition.
- `cdp.input.insertText` This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press, for example an emoji keyboard or an IME.
- `cdp.input.setIgnoreInputEvents` Ignores input events (useful while auditing page).
- `cdp.input.setInterceptDrags` Prevents default drag and drop behavior and instead emits `Input.dragIntercepted` events. Drag and drop behavior can be directly controlled via `Input.dispatchDragEvent`.
- `cdp.input.synthesizePinchGesture` Synthesizes a pinch gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.
- `cdp.input.synthesizeScrollGesture` Synthesizes a scroll gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.
- `cdp.input.synthesizeTapGesture` Synthesizes a tap gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

## Network — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.network.canClearBrowserCache` Tells whether clearing browser cache is supported.
- `cdp.network.canClearBrowserCookies` Tells whether clearing browser cookies is supported.
- `cdp.network.canEmulateNetworkConditions` Tells whether emulation of network conditions is supported.
- `cdp.network.clearAcceptedEncodingsOverride` Clears accepted encodings set by setAcceptedEncodings
- `cdp.network.clearBrowserCache` — **dangerous** Clears browser cache.
- `cdp.network.clearBrowserCookies` — **dangerous** Clears browser cookies.
- `cdp.network.configureDurableMessages` Configures storing response bodies outside of renderer, so that these survive a cross-process navigation. If maxTotalBufferSize is not set, durable messages are disabled.
- `cdp.network.continueInterceptedRequest` Response to Network.requestIntercepted which either modifies the request to continue with any modifications, or blocks it, or completes it with the provided response bytes. If a network fetch occurs as a result which encounters a redirect an additional Network.requestIntercepted event will be sent with the same InterceptionId. Deprecated, use Fetch.continueRequest, Fetch.fulfillRequest and Fetch.failRequest instead.
- `cdp.network.deleteCookies` Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path/partitionKey pair.
- `cdp.network.deleteDeviceBoundSession` Deletes a device bound session.
- `cdp.network.emulateNetworkConditions` Activates emulation of network conditions. This command is deprecated in favor of the emulateNetworkConditionsByRule and overrideNetworkState commands, which can be used together to the same effect.
- `cdp.network.emulateNetworkConditionsByRule` Activates emulation of network conditions for individual requests using URL match patterns. Unlike the deprecated Network.emulateNetworkConditions this method does not affect `navigator` state. Use Network.overrideNetworkState to explicitly modify `navigator` behavior.
- `cdp.network.enableDeviceBoundSessions` Sets up tracking device bound sessions and fetching of initial set of sessions.
- `cdp.network.enableReportingApi` Enables tracking for the Reporting API, events generated by the Reporting API will now be delivered to the client. Enabling triggers 'reportingApiReportAdded' for all existing reports.
- `cdp.network.fetchSchemefulSite` Fetches the schemeful site for a specific origin.
- `cdp.network.getAllCookies` Returns all browser cookies. Depending on the backend support, will return detailed cookie information in the `cookies` field. Deprecated. Use Storage.getCookies instead.
- `cdp.network.getCertificate` Returns the DER-encoded certificate.
- `cdp.network.getCookies` Returns all browser cookies for the current URL. Depending on the backend support, will return detailed cookie information in the `cookies` field.
- `cdp.network.getRequestPostData` Returns post data sent with the request. Returns an error when no data was sent with the request.
- `cdp.network.getResponseBody` Returns content served for the given request.
- `cdp.network.getResponseBodyForInterception` Returns content served for the given currently intercepted request.
- `cdp.network.getSecurityIsolationStatus` Returns information about the COEP/COOP isolation status.
- `cdp.network.loadNetworkResource` Fetches the resource and returns the content.
- `cdp.network.overrideNetworkState` Override the state of navigator.onLine and navigator.connection.
- `cdp.network.replayXHR` This method sends a new XMLHttpRequest which is identical to the original one. The following parameters should be identical: method, url, async, request body, extra headers, withCredentials attribute, user, password.
- `cdp.network.searchInResponseBody` Searches for given string in response content.
- `cdp.network.setAcceptedEncodings` Sets a list of content encodings that will be accepted. Empty list means no encoding is accepted.
- `cdp.network.setAttachDebugStack` Specifies whether to attach a page script stack id in requests
- `cdp.network.setBlockedURLs` Blocks URLs from loading.
- `cdp.network.setBypassServiceWorker` Toggles ignoring of service worker for each request.
- `cdp.network.setCacheDisabled` Toggles ignoring cache for each request. If `true`, cache will not be used.
- `cdp.network.setCookie` Sets a cookie with the given cookie data; may overwrite equivalent cookies if they exist.
- `cdp.network.setCookieControls` Sets Controls for third-party cookie access Page reload is required before the new cookie behavior will be observed
- `cdp.network.setCookies` Sets given cookies.
- `cdp.network.setExtraHTTPHeaders` Specifies whether to always send extra HTTP headers with the requests from this page.
- `cdp.network.setRequestInterception` Sets the requests to intercept that match the provided patterns and optionally resource types. Deprecated, please use Fetch.enable instead.
- `cdp.network.setUserAgentOverride` Allows overriding user agent with the given string.
- `cdp.network.streamResourceContent` Enables streaming of the response for the given requestId. If enabled, the dataReceived event contains the data that was received during streaming.
- `cdp.network.takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream` Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body. Note that after this command, the intercepted request can't be continued as is -- you either need to cancel it or to provide the response body. The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position is specified.

## Page — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.page.addCompilationCache` Seeds compilation cache for given url. Compilation cache does not survive cross-process navigation.
- `cdp.page.addScriptToEvaluateOnLoad` Deprecated, please use addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.
- `cdp.page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument` Evaluates given script in every frame upon creation (before loading frame's scripts).
- `cdp.page.bringToFront` Brings page to front (activates tab).
- `cdp.page.captureScreenshot` — **binary** (`data` → `image/png`) Capture page screenshot.
- `cdp.page.captureSnapshot` — **binary** (`data` → `multipart/related`) Returns a snapshot of the page as a string. For MHTML format, the serialization includes iframes, shadow DOM, external resources, and element-inline styles.
- `cdp.page.clearCompilationCache` Clears seeded compilation cache.
- `cdp.page.clearDeviceMetricsOverride` Clears the overridden device metrics.
- `cdp.page.clearDeviceOrientationOverride` Clears the overridden Device Orientation.
- `cdp.page.clearGeolocationOverride` Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.
- `cdp.page.close` Tries to close page, running its beforeunload hooks, if any.
- `cdp.page.crash` — **dangerous** Crashes renderer on the IO thread, generates minidumps.
- `cdp.page.createIsolatedWorld` Creates an isolated world for the given frame.
- `cdp.page.deleteCookie` Deletes browser cookie with given name, domain and path.
- `cdp.page.generateTestReport` Generates a report for testing.
- `cdp.page.getAdScriptAncestry`
- `cdp.page.getAnnotatedPageContent` Get the annotated page content for the main frame. This is an experimental command that is subject to change.
- `cdp.page.getAppId` Returns the unique (PWA) app id. Only returns values if the feature flag 'WebAppEnableManifestId' is enabled
- `cdp.page.getAppManifest` Gets the processed manifest for this current document. This API always waits for the manifest to be loaded. If manifestId is provided, and it does not match the manifest of the current document, this API errors out. If there is not a loaded page, this API errors out immediately.
- `cdp.page.getFrameTree` Returns present frame tree structure.
- `cdp.page.getInstallabilityErrors`
- `cdp.page.getLayoutMetrics` Returns metrics relating to the layouting of the page, such as viewport bounds/scale.
- `cdp.page.getManifestIcons` Deprecated because it's not guaranteed that the returned icon is in fact the one used for PWA installation.
- `cdp.page.getNavigationHistory` Returns navigation history for the current page.
- `cdp.page.getOriginTrials` Get Origin Trials on given frame.
- `cdp.page.getPermissionsPolicyState` Get Permissions Policy state on given frame.
- `cdp.page.getResourceContent` Returns content of the given resource.
- `cdp.page.getResourceTree` Returns present frame / resource tree structure.
- `cdp.page.handleJavaScriptDialog` Accepts or dismisses a JavaScript initiated dialog (alert, confirm, prompt, or onbeforeunload).
- `cdp.page.navigate` Navigates current page to the given URL.
- `cdp.page.navigateToHistoryEntry` Navigates current page to the given history entry.
- `cdp.page.printToPDF` — **binary** (`data` → `application/pdf`) Print page as PDF.
- `cdp.page.produceCompilationCache` Requests backend to produce compilation cache for the specified scripts. `scripts` are appended to the list of scripts for which the cache would be produced. The list may be reset during page navigation. When script with a matching URL is encountered, the cache is optionally produced upon backend discretion, based on internal heuristics. See also: `Page.compilationCacheProduced`.
- `cdp.page.reload` Reloads given page optionally ignoring the cache.
- `cdp.page.removeScriptToEvaluateOnLoad` Deprecated, please use removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.
- `cdp.page.removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument` Removes given script from the list.
- `cdp.page.resetNavigationHistory` Resets navigation history for the current page.
- `cdp.page.screencastFrameAck` Acknowledges that a screencast frame has been received by the frontend.
- `cdp.page.searchInResource` Searches for given string in resource content.
- `cdp.page.setAdBlockingEnabled` Enable Chrome's experimental ad filter on all sites.
- `cdp.page.setBypassCSP` Enable page Content Security Policy by-passing.
- `cdp.page.setDeviceMetricsOverride` Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height, window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and "device-width"/"device-height"-related CSS media query results).
- `cdp.page.setDeviceOrientationOverride` Overrides the Device Orientation.
- `cdp.page.setDocumentContent` Sets given markup as the document's HTML.
- `cdp.page.setDownloadBehavior` Set the behavior when downloading a file.
- `cdp.page.setFontFamilies` Set generic font families.
- `cdp.page.setFontSizes` Set default font sizes.
- `cdp.page.setGeolocationOverride` Overrides the Geolocation Position or Error. Omitting any of the parameters emulates position unavailable.
- `cdp.page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog` Intercept file chooser requests and transfer control to protocol clients. When file chooser interception is enabled, native file chooser dialog is not shown. Instead, a protocol event `Page.fileChooserOpened` is emitted.
- `cdp.page.setLifecycleEventsEnabled` Controls whether page will emit lifecycle events.
- `cdp.page.setPrerenderingAllowed` Enable/disable prerendering manually. This command is a short-term solution for https://crbug.com/1440085. See https://docs.google.com/document/d/12HVmFxYj5Jc-eJr5OmWsa2bqTJsbgGLKI6ZIyx0_wpA for more details. TODO(https://crbug.com/1440085): Remove this once Puppeteer supports tab targets.
- `cdp.page.setRPHRegistrationMode` Extensions for Custom Handlers API: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/system-state.html#rph-automation
- `cdp.page.setSPCTransactionMode` Sets the Secure Payment Confirmation transaction mode. https://w3c.github.io/secure-payment-confirmation/#sctn-automation-set-spc-transaction-mode
- `cdp.page.setTouchEmulationEnabled` Toggles mouse event-based touch event emulation.
- `cdp.page.setWebLifecycleState` Tries to update the web lifecycle state of the page. It will transition the page to the given state according to: https://github.com/WICG/web-lifecycle/
- `cdp.page.startScreencast` Starts sending each frame using the `screencastFrame` event.
- `cdp.page.stopLoading` Force the page stop all navigations and pending resource fetches.
- `cdp.page.stopScreencast` Stops sending each frame in the `screencastFrame`.
- `cdp.page.waitForDebugger` Pauses page execution. Can be resumed using generic Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger.

## Runtime — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.runtime.addBinding` If executionContextId is empty, adds binding with the given name on the global objects of all inspected contexts, including those created later, bindings survive reloads. Binding function takes exactly one argument, this argument should be string, in case of any other input, function throws an exception. Each binding function call produces Runtime.bindingCalled notification.
- `cdp.runtime.awaitPromise` Add handler to promise with given promise object id.
- `cdp.runtime.callFunctionOn` Calls function with given declaration on the given object. Object group of the result is inherited from the target object.
- `cdp.runtime.compileScript` Compiles expression.
- `cdp.runtime.discardConsoleEntries` Discards collected exceptions and console API calls.
- `cdp.runtime.evaluate` Evaluates expression on global object.
- `cdp.runtime.getExceptionDetails` This method tries to lookup and populate exception details for a JavaScript Error object. Note that the stackTrace portion of the resulting exceptionDetails will only be populated if the Runtime domain was enabled at the time when the Error was thrown.
- `cdp.runtime.getHeapUsage` Returns the JavaScript heap usage. It is the total usage of the corresponding isolate not scoped to a particular Runtime.
- `cdp.runtime.getIsolateId` Returns the isolate id.
- `cdp.runtime.getProperties` Returns properties of a given object. Object group of the result is inherited from the target object.
- `cdp.runtime.globalLexicalScopeNames` Returns all let, const and class variables from global scope.
- `cdp.runtime.queryObjects`
- `cdp.runtime.releaseObject` Releases remote object with given id.
- `cdp.runtime.releaseObjectGroup` Releases all remote objects that belong to a given group.
- `cdp.runtime.removeBinding` This method does not remove binding function from global object but unsubscribes current runtime agent from Runtime.bindingCalled notifications.
- `cdp.runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger` Tells inspected instance to run if it was waiting for debugger to attach.
- `cdp.runtime.runScript` Runs script with given id in a given context.
- `cdp.runtime.setAsyncCallStackDepth` Enables or disables async call stacks tracking.
- `cdp.runtime.setCustomObjectFormatterEnabled`
- `cdp.runtime.setMaxCallStackSizeToCapture`
- `cdp.runtime.terminateExecution` — **dangerous** Terminate current or next JavaScript execution. Will cancel the termination when the outer-most script execution ends.

## Storage — scope: browser

- `cdp.storage.clearCookies` — **dangerous** Clears cookies.
- `cdp.storage.clearDataForOrigin` — **dangerous** Clears storage for origin.
- `cdp.storage.clearDataForStorageKey` — **dangerous** Clears storage for storage key.
- `cdp.storage.clearSharedStorageEntries` Clears all entries for a given origin's shared storage.
- `cdp.storage.clearTrustTokens` Removes all Trust Tokens issued by the provided issuerOrigin. Leaves other stored data, including the issuer's Redemption Records, intact.
- `cdp.storage.deleteSharedStorageEntry` Deletes entry for `key` (if it exists) for a given origin's shared storage.
- `cdp.storage.deleteStorageBucket` Deletes the Storage Bucket with the given storage key and bucket name.
- `cdp.storage.getCookies` Returns all browser cookies.
- `cdp.storage.getInterestGroupDetails` Gets details for a named interest group.
- `cdp.storage.getRelatedWebsiteSets` Returns the effective Related Website Sets in use by this profile for the browser session. The effective Related Website Sets will not change during a browser session.
- `cdp.storage.getSharedStorageEntries` Gets the entries in an given origin's shared storage.
- `cdp.storage.getSharedStorageMetadata` Gets metadata for an origin's shared storage.
- `cdp.storage.getStorageKey` Returns storage key for the given frame. If no frame ID is provided, the storage key of the target executing this command is returned.
- `cdp.storage.getStorageKeyForFrame` Returns a storage key given a frame id. Deprecated. Please use Storage.getStorageKey instead.
- `cdp.storage.getTrustTokens` Returns the number of stored Trust Tokens per issuer for the current browsing context.
- `cdp.storage.getUsageAndQuota` Returns usage and quota in bytes.
- `cdp.storage.overrideQuotaForOrigin` Override quota for the specified origin
- `cdp.storage.resetSharedStorageBudget` Resets the budget for `ownerOrigin` by clearing all budget withdrawals.
- `cdp.storage.runBounceTrackingMitigations` Deletes state for sites identified as potential bounce trackers, immediately.
- `cdp.storage.setCookies` Sets given cookies.
- `cdp.storage.setInterestGroupAuctionTracking` Enables/Disables issuing of interestGroupAuctionEventOccurred and interestGroupAuctionNetworkRequestCreated.
- `cdp.storage.setInterestGroupTracking` Enables/Disables issuing of interestGroupAccessed events.
- `cdp.storage.setProtectedAudienceKAnonymity`
- `cdp.storage.setSharedStorageEntry` Sets entry with `key` and `value` for a given origin's shared storage.
- `cdp.storage.setSharedStorageTracking` Enables/disables issuing of sharedStorageAccessed events.
- `cdp.storage.setStorageBucketTracking` Set tracking for a storage key's buckets.
- `cdp.storage.trackCacheStorageForOrigin` Registers origin to be notified when an update occurs to its cache storage list.
- `cdp.storage.trackCacheStorageForStorageKey` Registers storage key to be notified when an update occurs to its cache storage list.
- `cdp.storage.trackIndexedDBForOrigin` Registers origin to be notified when an update occurs to its IndexedDB.
- `cdp.storage.trackIndexedDBForStorageKey` Registers storage key to be notified when an update occurs to its IndexedDB.
- `cdp.storage.untrackCacheStorageForOrigin` Unregisters origin from receiving notifications for cache storage.
- `cdp.storage.untrackCacheStorageForStorageKey` Unregisters storage key from receiving notifications for cache storage.
- `cdp.storage.untrackIndexedDBForOrigin` Unregisters origin from receiving notifications for IndexedDB.
- `cdp.storage.untrackIndexedDBForStorageKey` Unregisters storage key from receiving notifications for IndexedDB.

## Target — scope: browser

- `cdp.target.activateTarget` Activates (focuses) the target.
- `cdp.target.closeTarget` Closes the target. If the target is a page that gets closed too.
- `cdp.target.createBrowserContext` Creates a new empty BrowserContext. Similar to an incognito profile but you can have more than one.
- `cdp.target.createTarget` Creates a new page.
- `cdp.target.disposeBrowserContext` Deletes a BrowserContext. All the belonging pages will be closed without calling their beforeunload hooks.
- `cdp.target.getBrowserContexts` Returns all browser contexts created with `Target.createBrowserContext` method.
- `cdp.target.getDevToolsTarget` Gets the targetId of the DevTools page target opened for the given target (if any).
- `cdp.target.getTargetInfo` Returns information about a target.
- `cdp.target.getTargets` Retrieves a list of available targets.
- `cdp.target.openDevTools` Opens a DevTools window for the target.
- `cdp.target.setDiscoverTargets` Controls whether to discover available targets and notify via `targetCreated/targetInfoChanged/targetDestroyed` events.
- `cdp.target.setRemoteLocations` Enables target discovery for the specified locations, when `setDiscoverTargets` was set to `true`.

## WebAuthn — scope: target, autoEnable

- `cdp.webAuthn.addCredential` Adds the credential to the specified authenticator.
- `cdp.webAuthn.addVirtualAuthenticator` Creates and adds a virtual authenticator.
- `cdp.webAuthn.clearCredentials` Clears all the credentials from the specified device.
- `cdp.webAuthn.getCredential` Returns a single credential stored in the given virtual authenticator that matches the credential ID.
- `cdp.webAuthn.getCredentials` Returns all the credentials stored in the given virtual authenticator.
- `cdp.webAuthn.removeCredential` Removes a credential from the authenticator.
- `cdp.webAuthn.removeVirtualAuthenticator` Removes the given authenticator.
- `cdp.webAuthn.setAutomaticPresenceSimulation` Sets whether tests of user presence will succeed immediately (if true) or fail to resolve (if false) for an authenticator. The default is true.
- `cdp.webAuthn.setCredentialProperties` Allows setting credential properties. https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#sctn-automation-set-credential-properties
- `cdp.webAuthn.setResponseOverrideBits` Resets parameters isBogusSignature, isBadUV, isBadUP to false if they are not present.
- `cdp.webAuthn.setUserVerified` Sets whether User Verification succeeds or fails for an authenticator. The default is true.
